question.text: An NRI wants to purchase shares of a listed Indian company under PIS. If the aggregate NRI holding is already at 8% and the company has passed a board resolution but NOT a special resolution to raise the aggregate ceiling, what is the maximum additional shares all NRIs together can purchase?

answer.text: Up to 16% of paid-up capital (aggregate limit increased to 24%)
Up to 2% of paid-up capital (aggregate limit remains 10% without special resolution)
Up to 5% of paid-up capital per individual NRI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The aggregate ceiling for NRI investment under PIS is 10% of paid-up capital. To raise it to 24%, both a board resolution AND a special resolution of shareholders are required. Since only a board resolution has been passed (without the special resolution), the aggregate limit remains at 10%. With 8% already held, only 2% additional headroom remains for all NRIs combined. **The Trap:** A board resolution alone is not sufficient to raise the limit - a special resolution is also mandatory. Some candidates may assume the board resolution alone is enough. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Both steps are mandatory: board resolution + special resolution. If only one is done, the limit stays at 10%. Individual NRI limit remains 5% regardless.
